references.text: [Ca57] Cassels, J. W. S., \"{U}ber Basen der nat\"{u}rlichen Zahlenreihe. Abh. Math. Sem. Univ. Hamburg (1957), 247-257.

remarks.text:
Erd\H{o}s originally asked this for any basis (not necessarily minimal); such a basis was constructed by Cassels \cite{Ca57}.

Erd\H{o}s and Graham conjectured a negative answer to this question.

statement.text: Does there exist $A=\{a_1<a_2<\cdots\}\subset \mathbb{N}$ which is a minimal basis of order $2$ (i.e. every large integer is the sum of $2$ elements from $A$, and no proper subset of $A$ has this property), such that\[\lim_{k\to \infty}\frac{a_k}{k^2}=c\]for some $c\neq 0$?
